summary: Added a Chapter Evidence Vault inside the selected Path Map Chapter Archive record deck so each chapter surfaces deduped artifact previews, proof records, and outcome packets as direct Event Proof focus cards.

queries: evidence dashboard UX proof artifact cards audit trail design pattern
document evidence vault dashboard UI artifact preview cards UX
audit evidence management dashboard proof cards UX pattern
pattern_notes: Audit trails become more useful when events are filtered into role-specific queues and dashboard surfaces.
Dashboard cards should be concise, predictable, and styled consistently with surrounding controls.
Evidence packs work best as structured collections of records, documents, and technical artifacts rather than raw file dumps.

ui_contract: Render Chapter Evidence Vault inside the selected Chapter Archive record deck.
Prefer proof-like, artifact-backed, and outcome records from the selected chapter, then fill from lane-level artifact previews.
Show preview, artifact, and proof counts.
Expose each card as a direct data-path-event-focus control for the existing Event Proof lens.
Expose TRL and LOG actions without starting workers, sending messages, or mutating the snapshot.

red_green.red: pytest tests\test_agent_company_atlas_path_chapter_evidence_vault.py -q failed before frontend wiring on missing function pathChapterEvidenceItems.

browser_green_route.evidence: Rendered one Chapter Evidence Vault with four proof cards, two compact artifact previews capped at two lines each, 2 previews, 2 artifacts, 2 proofs, TRL/LOG actions, cache-busted CSS/JS, no horizontal overflow, and zero console errors.

event_focus_action.evidence: The first vault card resolved to exactly one data-path-event-focus target, clicked successfully, marked the matching chapter record active, and updated the existing Event Proof lens with no console errors.

responsive.evidence: All tested viewports rendered one Evidence Vault with four cards, two action buttons, two compact two-line artifact previews, no horizontal overflow, no bad rectangles, no clipped evidence text, and no console errors.
